Tests that the sine of negative infinity is NaN using an assertion.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that verifies the behavior of the `Math.sin` function when it receives `-Infinity` as an argument. It uses the `assert` function to check that the result of `Math.sin(-Infinity)` is `NaN` (Not-a-Number). This test ensures that the `Math.sin` function conforms to the expected mathematical behavior when handling negative infinity. The script is narrow in functionality, focusing solely on this specific edge case for the sine function.
